article_title: Claude Code Vulnerabilities Let Attackers Run Arbitrary Commands and Steal API Keys by Cloning a Repository

content_preview.title: Claude Code Vulnerabilities Let Attackers Run Arbitrary Commands and Steal API Keys by Cloning a Repository
content_preview.summary: Check Point Research disclosed two CVEs in Anthropic's Claude Code that turned project configuration files into attack vectors, enabling remote code execution and API key exfiltration before users could approve a trust dialog.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Check Point Research disclosed two critical vulnerabilities in Anthropic's Claude Code, the AI-powered command-line coding assistant, that allowed attackers to execute arbitrary shell commands on developers' machines and steal Anthropic API keys — triggered simply by cloning and opening a malicious repository, according to the [Check Point Research disclosure](https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/rce-and-api-token-exfiltration-through-claude-code-project-files-cve-2025-59536/). The ...

recommendations: Consider adding research.checkpoint.com and cybernews.com to the source allowlist — both are established cybersecurity outlets

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured cybersecurity News article at 688 words (within News range). Clear technical explanation of both CVEs without excessive jargon. Good chronological timeline section.
editor_notes.source_verification: 4 sources: Check Point Research (primary disclosure — the definitive source), The Hacker News (major cybersecurity outlet, on allowlist), Dark Reading (established infosec publication, 403 bot-block but known reputable), CyberNews (cybersecurity news outlet). Excellent primary sourcing from the actual vulnerability researchers.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: CVE numbers (CVE-2025-59536, CVE-2026-21852), GHSA reference (GHSA-ph6w-f82w-28w6), and timeline dates are specific and attributed to Check Point's disclosure. Technical details about Hooks, MCP servers, and ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL override are accurately described. The article correctly notes patches have been deployed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and factual throughout. No sensationalism despite covering vulnerabilities in the tool this newsroom itself uses. Reports both the vulnerabilities and Anthropic's mitigations fairly.
editor_notes.originality: No existing articles on these specific Claude Code CVEs. A previous RoguePilot article covered different GitHub Actions vulnerabilities. This is distinct.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong cybersecurity News article with excellent primary sourcing from Check Point Research. Technical accuracy, clear timeline, and balanced reporting. Ready for publication.
